page: Amazon Web Services m5.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance across multi-core and memory operations, contrasted with weaker single-core capabilities. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores fall within the middle 50% tier, while single-core metrics from Geekbench and stress-ng are positioned in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show average throughput, reaching 49.54 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 36.88 GB/sec for writes. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported by hardware-accelerated instructions such as AES and AVX-512.

Given its balanced resource allocation and average multi-core performance, the m5.2xlarge is suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and software compilation. Redis SET operations perform at an average rate of 6.65 million operations per second. However, the instance exhibits limitations in compute-intensive tasks, with weak performance in single-threaded decompression and poor results in medium-sized large language model inference. It represents a standard resource-dense option for applications requiring a stable balance of memory and processing power without local storage requirements.
description: Amazon Web Services m5.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, running on an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor at 3.1 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance and complex tasks like LLM inference are relatively weak. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, the m5.2xlarge is suited for standard workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations where a stable balance of compute and memory is required without the need for high-speed local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering balanced performance for standard enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, built on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine offering dedicated Intel Xeon processing and DDR4 memory for standard enterprise workloads.

Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor running at 3.1 GHz
